reason: The B2 comparative runset (n=3) reported H1 failing with pretable 11% above MUI on scroll_frame_p95_ms. The B2 follow-up #1 perf-diag rerun (n=20) shows the gap was sample noise: pretable 9.07ms ± 0.20 vs mui 9.14ms ± 0.19; mean diff -0.065ms is well inside the 2σ noise floor of 0.401ms. The original H1 flip did not survive higher-repeat scrutiny — pretable in fact comes in marginally faster on average.

correctedH1.summary: Corrected from B2 n=3 runset. At n=20 on Chromium S2/hypothesis, pretable's mean scroll_frame_p95_ms (9.07ms) is within the 10% parity band of MUI's mean (9.14ms) — and is in fact marginally faster. All H1 quality sub-criteria (zero blank gaps, zero anchor shift, ≤1px row-height error) remain satisfied. The original B2 'failing' verdict was a low-repeat sample artifact, not a real regression.

tanstackAndAgGridUnchanged.note: AG Grid (16.7ms p95, 1 blank gap, 2px row-height drift) and TanStack (16.7ms p95, 1 blank gap) status from the B2 n=3 runset is not corrected here — both adapters were measured at large enough deltas (>50% above pretable) that low-repeat noise is unlikely to flip them. They remain ~1.7× pretable's scroll_frame_p95_ms with quality gaps that pretable does not have. A future high-repeat run including those adapters could refine the multipliers but would not change the qualitative conclusion.
